records.specific_details: Advocating for fair, proportionate and accessible funding for the public service content on independent radio stations in the Budget and in policy discussions. Seeking level playing field.
records.intended_results: Inclusion of accessible funding for independent radio sector in Budget, as well as measures to ensure existing funding could be accessed. Fair share of Sound and Vision funding to be allocated to independent radio.::Measures to ensure a level playing field in regulatory and legislative terms for independent stations and fair application of State Aid in the media landscape, as well as unrestricted and prominent access for listeners to radio in vehicles or on devices or platforms. ::Budget allocation for accessible funding for investment in capital and maintenance of emergency communications transmission equipment.::Attendance invited for event aimed at highlighting need to protect prominent access for radio, and arrangement of meetings and highlighting IBI policy priorities

records.lobbyist_name: National Infertility Support and Information Group
records.specific_details: NISIG emailed members of the Oireachtas our 2026 pre-budget submission. Key asks in the pre-budget submission included funding our NISIG Helpline, a request for a wider and more sufficiently funded public funded IVF scheme. Also, that eligibility and criteria be broad, equitable, inclusive and reflective of those who seek infertility treatment. Also, that donor conception must be ready to roll out in conjunction with the establishment of the new Regulatory Authority
records.intended_results: To secure future funding for the NISIG Helpline, to maintain this crucial support for those in need of assistance particularly at times of great emotional stress.::NISIG are calling for a wider and more sufficiently funded public funding system for IVF including significantly increased funding for the fertility hubs nationwide::NISIG asks that eligibility and criteria for public funding for IVF are broad, equitable, inclusive and reflective of those who seek infertility treatment.  This includes a review of the current criteria based on data from the last 2 years, along with areas such as funding for patients requiring PGT-M and PGT-SR, and additional cycles of IVF for couples with no children. Donor conception must be ready to roll out in conjunction with the establishment of the new Regulatory Authority. ::There is no provision for specific support by the State health services for those going through fertility treatment and NISIG receives no Government funding for the vital support s

records.specific_details: Mercosur Trade Deal
records.intended_results: That the Government and MEPs must honour their commitments to stand up for farmers and defend their interests by opposing the current Mercosur trade deal.::To discuss farmers' concerns with the Minister for Foreign Affairs::To reiterate strong opposition to the Mercosur agreement in its current form. The beef industry in Ireland supports 90,000 farmers and around 25,000 jobs and is critical to the rural economy. There are very strong concerns about the impact the EU-Mercosur trade agreement would have on the viability of some 90,000 families.::That, while it is often argued that an additional 99,000t of beef from the Mercosur block countries would represent only 1-2% of the EU beef market, this statistic is in fact very misleading. The high value EU steak market (fillet, striploin, and cube) will be targeted by Mercosur beef exporters and will impact EU producers' ability to achieve a positive return on the carcass. 30% of the value of the carcass is contained within the yield of high

records.specific_details: The lobbying intent was to raise awareness of our submission to the Department of Sport of our ask to make a Special Allocation in Budget 2026.  The ask was for €5m to fully renovate and restore a derelict building in Dun Laoghaire for use as a National Sailing Performance HQ.  This new building will replace the shipping containers which are currently in use, but which have no toilet, shower or changing facilities.
records.intended_results: The intended result was to raise awareness and understanding of our ask in order that the relevant ministers would support this in the pre-budget allocation

records.specific_details: Budget 2026 priorities for telecommunications sector
records.intended_results: Expansion of the remit of the Grow Digital Voucher for SMEs to include the costs of enabling fibre and 5G FWA Very High Capacity Network connectivity; ::Funding of a fit for purpose mobile public warning system as done in Denmark, France, Germany, Spain, and the UK; ::Funding  for a multiannual plan to increase the redundancy and security of Ireland’s subsea cables; ::Support for the delivery of fibre broadband to premises in fibre broadband “blackspots” outside the National Broadband Plan intervention area; ::Use of spectrum fees and receipts from spectrum auctions collected from industry to fund a reduction in mobile coverage ‘blackspots’;::Upgrading of monitored personal alarm devices provided under the Senior Alert Scheme so that they incorporate mobile back up and can function over the fibre network; ::Reduction of the fixed cost component on energy bills.

records.specific_details: Responded to correspondence from Patrick O'Donovan, Minister for Arts & Media in which he declined to meet with Uplift members on the People's Inquiry and urged us to talk to Coimisiún na Meán. I said the issue was pressing for government and extended beyond Coimisiún na Meán's scope into issues such as AI and GDPR and asked him to reconsider and meet with us.
records.intended_results: That a meeting would be secured with Minister Patrick O'Donovan to discuss the findings in the report from Uplift's People's Inquiry into Big Tech Harms.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science

lobbying_activities: One email was sent to Minister O'Donovan - Email

records.specific_details: Uplift members and staff members went to Patrick O'Donovan's constituency office in Newcastle West to deliver a copy of the report that came out of Uplift's People's Inquiry on Big Tech Harms. He wasn't there so we put it through his letter box and put posters up on his office window calling on him to implement the recommendations in our report. We also handed out leaflets in the town and put up content on socials. We also delivered a letter to his office.
records.intended_results: That Patrick O'Donovan take big tech harms seriously, agree to meet with us, and agree to implement recommendations from our People's Inquiry report.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media

lobbying_activities: We posted a letter through he letterbox at Patrick O'Donovan's constituency office in Newcastle West, along with a copy of the report from Uplift's People's Inquiry into Big Tech Harms. - Letter

records.specific_details: Uplift members emailed members of the Committee on Foreign Affairs and Trade and asked them to perform full pre-legislative scrutiny of the changes to the Arbitration Act that would allow the Government to ratify the CETA trade deal. Uplift members also emailed members of the cabinet asking them asking them not to give cabinet approval to amend the Arbitration Act.
records.intended_results: That the Committee on Foreign Affairs and Trade would not waive crucial pre-legislative scrutiny on changes to the Arbitration act, making it harder for the Government to ratify the CETA trade deal. That cabinet approval would be withheld for amending the Arbitration Act.

records.intended_results: To ensure an understanding of IPOA perspectives on proposal for reform to the rental market regulatory environment.

lobbying_activities: Note: this is over multiple meetings and not all NBI officials or Designated Public Officials were present at the same time. - Meeting

Note:this is over multiple meetings, including engagements at the National Ploughing Championships and not all NBI officials or Designated Public Officials were present at the same time. - Informal communication

records.specific_details: The Indecon Report on the Economic and Social Impact of GPA Players and how its findings relate to State funding for inter-county players
records.intended_results: To ensure there is a understanding of the financial burden faced by inter-county players at a time they are generating significant sums for the exchequer.  This is part of a campaign to seek an increase in the government grant available to inter-county players as part of the upcoming budget.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform
Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media
Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media

lobbying_activities: August 27th: Email to Ireland's 14 MEPs inviting them to an online briefing on the Indecon Report. - Email
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Jack Chambers TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Patrick O'Donovan TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission

July 17th: Meeting with Fianna Fáil representatives facilitates by Paul McAuliffe TD, Fianna Fáil (Chair of Oireachtas Housing Committee) at Leinster House.  Other attendees included John Lahart TD and Cormac Devlin TD.  Other individuals came in and out of the meeting over the course of the hours.  Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley were joined at the meeting by Dublin players Brian Howard, Eve O’Brien, Eoin Murchan and Conor Burke.  Brendan Walsh from Blueyard also attended. - Meeting

July 2nd: Meeting with Seán Canney TD and Padraig Hughes at Leinster House.  This meeting was attended by Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley from the GPA.  they were joined by Galway hurler Conor Whelan and Mandy Johnston from Blueyard. - Meeting
June 11th: Email to Patrick Leonard at the Department of Transport requesting a meeting with Sean Canney TD from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
June 27th: Email to Alan Kelly TD to request an opportunity to present the findings of the Indecon Report to the Oireachtas Committee on Sport.  This email came from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
May 15th: Meeting with Minister Jack Chambers TD.  Tom Parsons attended the meeting on behalf of the GPA at the Department of Finance - Meeting

May 20th: Meeting with Minister Patrick O'Donovan TD, Theresa Newman and Ciaran Shanley at the Department of Sport. Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley attended this meeting on behalf of the GPA and were joined by GPA National Executive Committee member Neil McManus. - Meeting

records.lobbyist_name: The Association of Animation Producers in Ireland Ltd
records.specific_details: Ronan McCabe met Minister O'Donovan at EXPO in Osaka to discuss regional incentives, a potential Ireland/Japan co production treaty and the lack of domestic finance available to animation studios. The Minister was invited to open the Irish Animation Awards on the 24th May in Galway. The Minister accepted the invitation.
records.intended_results: The introduction of regional production incentives for animation.To start the process to putting an Ireland/Japan co-production treaty in place.To review the lack of a functioning banking sector for the creative sector.
lobbying_activities: Ronan McCabe met the Minister at EXPO Osaka and the Minister opened the Irish Animation Awards and was introduced to the Animation Ireland board. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Post Office Network
records.intended_results: To secure the viability of the Post Office Network in Ireland

Minister Charlie McConalogue opened the Irish Postmaster's Union Annual Conference on 10th May in the Tullamore Court Hotel - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Cork Chamber Pre-Budget Submission 2026
records.intended_results: To advocate for measures to ensure the achievement of housing targets in the Cork region, including innovative housing solutions such as above the shop living and targeted financial supports for measures to stimulate the housing market.

records.specific_details: Cork Chamber Pre-Budget Submission 2026
records.intended_results: To call for progress on key transport projects in the Cork region and further afield in the context of Budget 2026, including regional connectivity via Cork Airport and the Port of Cork, strategic rail links and the Cork Area Commuter Rail Programme, as well as progress on critical roads projects and public transport infrastructure, such as BusConnects Cork and the Cork Luas.
